1→//
     2→//  FirestoreExerciseService.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Fetches exercises from the Firestore catalog collection.
     6→//  Primary exercise data source for the app.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→import FirebaseFirestore
    11→
    12→/// Firestore-backed exercise service.
    13→actor FirestoreExerciseService: ExerciseServiceProtocol {
    14→    private let db = Firestore.firestore()
    15→    private let collectionName = "exercises"
    16→
    17→    /// In-memory cache: [exerciseId: CatalogExercise]
    18→    private var cache: [String: CatalogExercise] = [:]
    19→
    20→    // MARK: - ExerciseServiceProtocol
    21→
    22→    func fetchExercises(
    23→        language: ExerciseLanguageCode,
    24→        category: String?,
    25→        equipment: [Int]?,
    26→        limit: Int
    27→    ) async throws -> [CatalogExercise] {
    28→        var query: Query = db.collection(collectionName)
    29→
    30→        if let category {
    31→            query = query.whereField("category", isEqualTo: category)
    32→        }
    33→
    34→        if let equipment, let firstEquipment = equipment.first {
    35→            query = query.whereField("equipment", arrayContains: firstEquipment)
    36→        }
    37→
    38→        query = query.limit(to: limit)
    39→
    40→        let snapshot = try await query.getDocuments()
    41→        let exercises = snapshot.documents.compactMap { doc -> CatalogExercise? in
    42→            let exercise = parseCatalogExercise(from: doc, language: language)
    43→            if let exercise { cache[exercise.id] = exercise }
    44→            return exercise
    45→        }
    46→
    47→        #if DEBUG
    48→        print("[FirestoreExerciseService] Fetched \(exercises.count) exercises (category: \(category ?? "all"))")
    49→        #endif
    50→
    51→        return exercises
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    func fetchExercise(id: String) async throws -> CatalogExercise? {
    55→        if let cached = cache[id] {
    56→            return cached
    57→        }
    58→
    59→        let doc = try await db.collection(collectionName).document(id).getDocument()
    60→
    61→        guard doc.exists else { return nil }
    62→
    63→        let exercise = parseCatalogExercise(from: doc, language: .portuguese)
    64→        if let exercise { cache[exercise.id] = exercise }
    65→        return exercise
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    func searchExercises(
    69→        query: String,
    70→        language: ExerciseLanguageCode,
    71→        limit: Int
    72→    ) async throws -> [CatalogExercise] {
    73→        // Firestore doesn't support full-text search natively.
    74→        // Use prefix matching on the name field.
    75→        let normalizedQuery = query.lowercased()
    76→            .folding(options: .diacriticInsensitive, locale: .current)
    77→
    78→        let snapshot = try await db.collection(collectionName)
    79→            .order(by: "name")
    80→            .start(at: [normalizedQuery])
    81→            .end(at: [normalizedQuery + "\u{f8ff}"])
    82→            .limit(to: limit)
    83→            .getDocuments()
    84→
    85→        let exercises = snapshot.documents.compactMap { doc -> CatalogExercise? in
    86→            let exercise = parseCatalogExercise(from: doc, language: language)
    87→            if let exercise { cache[exercise.id] = exercise }
    88→            return exercise
    89→        }
    90→
    91→        #if DEBUG
    92→        print("[FirestoreExerciseService] Search '\(query)' → \(exercises.count) results")
    93→        #endif
    94→
    95→        return exercises
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    // MARK: - Parsing
    99→
   100→    private func parseCatalogExercise(from doc: DocumentSnapshot, language: ExerciseLanguageCode) -> CatalogExercise? {
   101→        guard let data = doc.data() else { return nil }
   102→
   103→        let name = resolveExerciseName(from: data, documentId: doc.documentID, language: language)
   104→        let description = resolveExerciseDescription(from: data, language: language)
   105→
   106→        #if DEBUG
   107→        if data["name"] == nil && data["name_\(language.code)"] == nil {
   108→            print("[FirestoreExerciseService] ⚠️ No name field found for \(doc.documentID). Keys: \(data.keys.sorted())")
   109→        }
   110→        #endif
   111→
   112→        return CatalogExercise(
   113→            id: doc.documentID,
   114→            name: name,
   115→            description: description,
   116→            category: data["category"] as? String,
   117→            muscles: data["muscles"] as? [Int] ?? [],
   118→            musclesSecondary: data["musclesSecondary"] as? [Int] ?? [],
   119→            equipment: data["equipment"] as? [Int] ?? []
   120→        )
   121→    }
   122→
   123→    /// Resolves the exercise name trying language-specific fields first, then generic name,
   124→    /// then falls back to a human-readable form of the document ID.
   125→    private func resolveExerciseName(from data: [String: Any], documentId: String, language: ExerciseLanguageCode) -> String {
   126→        // Try language-specific name (e.g., "name_pt", "name_en")
   127→        if let localizedName = data["name_\(language.code)"] as? String, !localizedName.isEmpty {
   128→            return localizedName
   129→        }
   130→        // Try generic "name" field
   131→        if let name = data["name"] as? String, !name.isEmpty {
   132→            return name
   133→        }
   134→        // Fallback: convert document ID to readable name (e.g., "barbell_bench_press" → "Barbell Bench Press")
   135→        return documentId
   136→            .replacingOccurrences(of: "_", with: " ")
   137→            .capitalized
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    /// Resolves the exercise description trying language-specific fields first.
   141→    private func resolveExerciseDescription(from data: [String: Any], language: ExerciseLanguageCode) -> String? {
   142→        if let localized = data["description_\(language.code)"] as? String, !localized.isEmpty {
   143→            return localized
   144→        }
   145→        return data["description"] as? String
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    // MARK: - Cache
   149→
   150→    func clearCache() {
   151→        cache.removeAll()
   152→    }
   153→}
   154→

content.thinking:
The issue is clear now. The exercise IDs like `barbell_bench_press_nb`, `alternating_floor_press`, `arm_circles`, `bag_training`, `claps_over_the_head` are coming from Firestore document IDs. When the app fetches exercises by category from Firestore, the document IDs are used as exercise IDs (line 113: `id: doc.documentID`), and then those IDs are used to build Firebase Storage paths.

The problem is that the Firestore `exercises` collection has documents with IDs that don't match the Storage folder names. This is a Firestore data issue, not an app code issue.


Even though the user confirmed the CMS upload was successful, there's likely a mismatch between how the CMS generates exercise IDs (possibly with suffixes like `_nb`) and where the images actually ended up in Storage. The app code itself is working correctly—it's just looking for images in paths that don't exist because the Firestore document IDs don't align with the Storage folder structure.

The most practical fix would be adding a `storage_id` field to the Firestore documents that points to the correct Storage path, rather than trying to rename existing documents or folders. Now I need to figure out what the actual Storage structure looks like and whether the exercise IDs from the logs match any real folders there.

Looking at how the exerciseId flows through the system: the Firestore document ID becomes the `CatalogExercise.id`, which then gets passed to the image cache to construct the path `exercises/{exerciseId}/media/{imageIndex}.jpg`. The problem is clear now—the Firestore IDs like `barbell_bench_press_nb` don't match what's actually in Storage, so the image lookups fail. This is fundamentally a data mismatch between the two systems.

I could solve this a few ways: add an optional `image_id` field to Firestore documents that maps to the correct Storage folder, or strip common suffixes like `_nb` in the image cache lookup logic. The most practical approach would be to add that mapping field to Firestore and fall back to the document ID if it's missing, though that does require updating the database. For now, the suffix-stripping approach might be quicker to implement and test.

content.text:
Agora está claro o problema. Vou explicar:

## Diagnóstico

Os exercise IDs vêm do **Firestore document ID** (linha 113 de `FirestoreExerciseService.swift`):
```swift
id: doc.documentID  // → "barbell_bench_press_nb", "arm_circles", etc.
```

O `ExerciseImageCache` usa esse ID para construir o path no Storage:
```
exercises/{doc.documentID}/media/0.jpg
```

Então se o documento no Firestore se chama `barbell_bench_press_nb`, o app procura:
```
exercises/barbell_bench_press_nb/media/0.jpg
```

**O problema é que os document IDs no Firestore não correspondem aos nomes das pastas no Firebase Storage.** Os IDs como `barbell_bench_press_nb`, `alternating_floor_press`, `arm_circles`, `bag_training`, `claps_over_the_head` não existem nos seed files do app — eles vêm diretamente da collection `exercises` no Firestore.

    11→
    12→/// Actor thread-safe para cache de imagens de exercícios do Firebase Storage.
    13→/// Dois níveis: memória (NSCache) + disco (Caches/exercise_images/).

    15→actor ExerciseImageCache {

    17→
    18→    // MARK: - Memory Cache

    25→    private let fileManager = FileManager.default
    26→

    30→
    31→    // MARK: - Configuration

    33→    private let maxDownloadSize: Int64 = 5 * 1024 * 1024 // 5MB
    34→    private let compressionQuality: CGFloat = 0.85
    35→    private let pruneThresholdDays: Int = 30
    36→

    38→
    39→    private init() {
    40→        let cacheDir = fileManager.urls(for: .cachesDirectory, in: .userDomainMask).first!
    41→        self.diskDirectory = cacheDir.appendingPathComponent("exercise_images", isDirectory: true)
    42→
    43→        // Create disk directory
    44→        try? fileManager.createDirectory(at: diskDirectory, withIntermediateDirectories: true)
    45→
    46→        // Configure memory cache limits

    48→        memoryCache.totalCostLimit = 50 * 1024 * 1024 // 50MB
    49→    }
    50→
    51→    // MARK: - Public API
    52→
    53→    /// Fetches an exercise image. Checks memory → disk → Firebase Storage.
    54→    /// - Parameters:
    55→    ///   - exerciseId: The exercise identifier
    56→    ///   - imageIndex: The image index (0 or 1)
    57→    /// - Returns: UIImage if found, nil otherwise
    58→    func image(for exerciseId: String, imageIndex: Int = 0) async -> UIImage? {
    59→        let key = cacheKey(exerciseId: exerciseId, imageIndex: imageIndex)
    60→
    61→        // 1. Memory cache
    62→        if let cached = memoryCache.object(forKey: key as NSString) {
    63→            return cached
    64→        }
    65→
    66→        // 2. Disk cache
    67→        if let diskImage = loadFromDisk(key: key) {
    68→            memoryCache.setObject(diskImage, forKey: key as NSString)
    69→            return diskImage
    70→        }
    71→
    72→        // 3. Firebase Storage download (deduplicated)
    73→        return await downloadFromStorage(exerciseId: exerciseId, imageIndex: imageIndex, key: key)
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    /// Prefetches images for all exercises in a workout using TaskGroup.
    77→    /// Downloads image 0 and image 1 for each exercise in parallel.
    78→    func prefetchWorkoutImages(exerciseIds: [String]) async {
    79→        await withTaskGroup(of: Void.self) { group in
    80→            for exerciseId in exerciseIds {
    81→                group.addTask {
    82→                    _ = await self.image(for: exerciseId, imageIndex: 0)
    83→                }
    84→                group.addTask {
    85→                    _ = await self.image(for: exerciseId, imageIndex: 1)
    86→                }
    87→            }
    88→        }
    89→    }
    90→
    91→    /// Removes cache files not accessed in the last 30 days.
    92→    func pruneOldCache() {
    93→        guard let files = try? fileManager.contentsOfDirectory(
    94→            at: diskDirectory,
    95→            includingPropertiesForKeys: [.contentAccessDateKey]
    96→        ) else { return }
    97→
    98→        let threshold = Calendar.current.date(byAdding: .day, value: -pruneThresholdDays, to: Date()) ?? Date()
    99→
   100→        for file in files {
   101→            guard let values = try? file.resourceValues(forKeys: [.contentAccessDateKey]),
   102→                  let accessDate = values.contentAccessDate else {
   103→                continue
   104→            }
   105→            if accessDate < threshold {

   107→            }
   108→        }
   109→
   110→        #if DEBUG
   111→        print("[ExerciseImageCache] pruneOldCache completed")
   112→        #endif
   113→    }
   114→
   115→    /// Removes all cached exercise images from memory and disk.
   116→    func clearCache() {

   119→        guard let files = try? fileManager.contentsOfDirectory(at: diskDirectory, includingPropertiesForKeys: nil) else {
   120→            return
   121→        }

   131→    // MARK: - Private
   132→
   133→    private func cacheKey(exerciseId: String, imageIndex: Int) -> String {

   136→
   137→    private func diskPath(for key: String) -> URL {

   140→
   141→    private func loadFromDisk(key: String) -> UIImage? {
   142→        let path = diskPath(for: key)
   143→        guard fileManager.fileExists(atPath: path.path) else { return nil }

   145→        // Update access time

   154→
   155→    private func saveToDisk(image: UIImage, key: String) {

3. **Extensão/formato diferente** — as imagens foram uploadadas com outro nome (ex: `image_0.jpg` em vez de `0.jpg`)

content.text:
Achei o problema! Olhe a **Storage rule** na linha 80:

```
match /exercises/{exerciseId}/{fileName} {
```

Essa rule só faz match com **2 níveis** de path: `exercises/{exerciseId}/{fileName}`. Mas o app está buscando **3 níveis**: `exercises/{exerciseId}/media/{imageIndex}.jpg` (com a subpasta `media/`).

A rule NÃO cobre o path `exercises/barbell_bench_press_nb/media/0.jpg` porque `media/0.jpg` tem 2 segmentos, não 1.

Confirmado. A rule na linha 80 faz match com `exercises/{exerciseId}/{fileName}` — isso é **2 wildcards** (2 segmentos de path). Mas o app busca `exercises/{exerciseId}/media/{imageIndex}.jpg` que tem **3 segmentos** (`exerciseId` + `media` + `0.jpg`).

O Firebase Storage rules não suporta match recursivo com wildcards simples. A rule precisa cobrir todos os sub-níveis. A correção é usar `{allPaths=**}` para cobrir subpastas.

content.text:
## Causa raiz encontrada e corrigida

A **Storage rule** estava com path matching errado:

**Antes** (linha 80):
```
match /exercises/{exerciseId}/{fileName} {
```
Isso só faz match com paths de 2 segmentos como `exercises/barbell_bench_press/0.jpg`, mas o app busca paths de 3 segmentos como `exercises/barbell_bench_press/media/0.jpg`.

**Depois**:
```
match /exercises/{exerciseId}/{allPaths=**} {
```
O `{allPaths=**}` faz match recursivo, cobrindo qualquer sub-path como `media/0.jpg` ou `thumbnail/0.webp`.

### Próximo passo

Você precisa **deploy das storage rules atualizadas** para o Firebase:
```bash
firebase deploy --only storage
```

Depois disso as imagens devem carregar corretamente para todos os exercícios que existem no Storage.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Firebase Storage path structure**: `exercises/{exerciseId}/media/{imageIndex}.jpg` (primary) and `exercises/{exerciseId}/thumbnail/{imageIndex}.webp` (fallback)
   - **Firebase Storage Rules**: Wildcard matching — `{fileName}` only matches one path segment, `{allPaths=**}` matches recursively
   - **ExerciseImageCache** (`actor`): Three-tier cache — memory (NSCache) → disk (Caches/exercise_images/) → Firebase Storage
   - **Exercise IDs**: Firestore `doc.documentID` is used directly as Storage folder name; IDs from Wger import have suffixes like `_nb`
   - **ProgramCategory inference**: Computed property in `ProgramModels.swift` uses `lowerId.contains()` to infer category from program ID
   - **LibraryWorkoutsSeed.json**: 16 workout templates (11 original + 5 new) referenced by programs via `workout_template_ids`
   - **Hero images**: Only 12 exist in Assets.xcassets (`program_fullbody_01–04`, `program_recovery_01–04`, `program_strength_01–04`)

3. Files and Code Sections:

     - Replaced 26 programs with exactly 12, each using only existing hero images

     - Added 5 new templates at end of file:

       - `lib_lower_beginner_gym` (leg_press, leg_curl_lying, leg_extension, goblet_squat, standing_calf_raise, glute_bridge)

       - `lib_lower_strength_gym` (barbell_squat, romanian_deadlift, hack_squat, leg_curl_lying, leg_extension, standing_calf_raise)

     - Single fix: replaced opaque Firestore ID `41n2hZZdH9uyYFGZ` with descriptive `lever_pec_deck_fly`

   - **`/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Domain/Entities/ProgramModels.swift`** (~L173)
     - Added `lowerId.contains("conditioning")` and `lowerName.contains("condicionamento")` to `.fatLoss` category check:
     ```swift

        lowerId.contains("conditioning") ||
        lowerName.contains("hiit") || lowerName.contains("queima") ||
        lowerName.contains("metabolic") || lowerName.contains("fat burn") ||
        lowerName.contains("condicionamento") {
         return .fatLoss
     }
     ```

   - **`/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/storage.rules`**
     - **Root cause fix for image loading errors**
     - Old (broken):
     ```
     match /exercises/{exerciseId}/{fileName} {
       allow read: if isAuthenticated();
     ```
     - New (fixed):
     ```
     match /exercises/{exerciseId}/{allPaths=**} {

       allow write: if false;
     }
     ```
     - The single-segment wildcard `{fileName}` only matched `exercises/{id}/file.jpg` but NOT `exercises/{id}/media/0.jpg` (3 segments). The recursive `{allPaths=**}` covers all sub-paths.

     - 5 tasks tracking the implementation work

   - **`/Users/viniciuscarvalho/Documents/FitToday/FitToday/FitToday/Data/Services/ImageCache/ExerciseImageCache.swift`** (READ ONLY)
     - Confirmed path structure: `exercises/\(exerciseId)/media/\(imageIndex).jpg`
     - `actor` with memory + disk + Firebase Storage three-tier cache
     - Uses `doc.documentID` as exerciseId directly

8. Current Work:

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: fix Storage rules recursive path and improve program cards UI

- storage.rules: replace single-segment {fileName} wildcard with
  {allPaths=**} so exercises/{id}/media/0.jpg paths are matched
  (fixes exercise images not loading from Firebase Storage)
- ProgramsListView/ProgramDetailView: use program.id hash for stable
  per-program image selection instead of per-goalTag; improve text
  overlay with ultraThinMaterial + stronger gradient for readability
EOF
)"

content.text:
Commit feito. Resumo do que foi incluído:

- **`storage.rules`** — fix crítico: `{fileName}` → `{allPaths=**}` para cobrir caminhos recursivos como `exercises/{id}/media/0.jpg`
- **`ProgramsListView.swift`** + **`ProgramDetailView.swift`** — seleção de imagem por hash do `program.id` (estável por programa) + overlay de texto com `ultraThinMaterial` e gradiente mais forte para melhor legibilidade

> **Lembrete**: para o fix do Storage rules entrar em produção, é necessário rodar `firebase deploy --only storage`.

content.content:
{"body":"# Design System — Nova Identidade: Constância & Equilíbrio Mental

## Contexto

O tema atual (`FitTodayColor` em `DesignTokens.swift`) é "Purple Futuristic / Retro-Synthwave" — cores neon agressivas, backgrounds roxo escuro, fontes Orbitron/Bungee com tracking alto, efeitos VHS e scanlines. Esse visual comunica adrenalina e high-performance, mas **não reflete o posicionamento atual do app**: constância e equilíbrio mental.

A nova paleta é mais calma, legível e acolhedora — ainda dark, mas sem o peso visual das neons.

---

## Nova Paleta de Cores

| Token | Hex Atual | Hex Novo | Intenção |
|---|---|---|---|
| `brandPrimary` | `#7C3AED` (Roxo) | **`#3B82F6`** | Ação, botões, CTAs — confiança e clareza |
| `brandSecondary` | `#A78BFA` | **`#60A5FA`** | Variante mais clara do azul |
| `brandAccent` | `#5B21B6` | **`#FB7185`** | Coral/Rosa — esforço, calorias, destaque quente |
| `background` | `#0D0D14` (roxo escuro) | **`#111111`** | Preto neutro sem tinte de cor |
| `backgroundElevated` | `#1A1A28` | **`#1A1A1A`** | Neutro puro |
| `surface` | `#1E1E2E` | **`#1E1E1E`** | Cards e superfícies |
| `surfaceElevated` | `#24243A` | **`#252525`** | Cards elevados |
| `textSecondary` | `#A0A0B8` | **`#94A3B8`** | Cinza Lavanda — reduz fadiga ocular |
| `textTertiary` | `#64648C` | **`#64748B`** | Cinza slate para infos terciárias |
| `outline` | `#2A2A3C` | **`#2A2A2A`** | Bordas neutras |
| `outlineVariant` | `#3D3D52` | **`#3A3A3A`** | Bordas mais visíveis |

**Cores de status permanecem (não mudam):**
- `success`: `#22C55E`
- `warning`: `#F59E0B`
- `error`: `#EF4444`
- `info`: `#3B82F6` → agora também é o `brandPrimary` (unificado)

---

## Gradientes

### Substituir
```swift
// ANTES — Purple vibrante
static let gradientPrimary = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#7C3AED"), Color(hex: "#5B21B6")], ...
)

// DEPOIS — Azul calmo
static let gradientPrimary = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#3B82F6"), Color(hex: "#1D4ED8")], ...
)

// Novo gradiente de acento (esforço/calorias)
static let gradientAccent = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#FB7185"), Color(hex: "#F43F5E")], ...
)

// Gradiente sutil para backgrounds de seção
static let gradientSurface = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#1A1A1A"), Color(hex: "#111111")], ...
)
```

### Gradientes de categoria de programa (ajustar para nova paleta)
- **Strength** → `#3B82F6` → `#1D4ED8` (azul primário, força/confiança)
- **Conditioning** → manter laranja `#F97316` → `#C2410C`
- **Aerobic** → `#FB7185` → `#F43F5E` (coral/rosa, esforço)
- **Endurance** → `#60A5FA` → `#3B82F6` (azul claro)
- **Wellness** → manter verde `#22C55E` → `#15803D`

---

## Legado para Remover / Deprecar

Os tokens abaixo pertencem ao visual futurista e **não fazem sentido** na nova identidade de equilíbrio mental:

```swift
// REMOVER — neons agressivos
static let neonCyan = Color(hex: "#00E5FF")
static let neonMagenta = Color(hex: "#FF00E5")
static let neonYellow = Color(hex: "#FFEA00")
static let neonPurple = Color(hex: "#B026FF")

// REMOVER — elementos de grid tech
static let gridLine = ...
static let gridAccent = ...
static let scanLine = ...
static let techBorder = ...

// REMOVER — efeitos VHS/Glitch
static let glitchRed = ...
static let glitchCyan = ...

// REMOVER — gradientes retro
static let gradientRetroSunset = ...
static let gradientSynthwave = ...
static let gradientNeonGlow = ...
```

---

## Extensões de View — Remover efeitos retro

Os métodos de `View` abaixo também saem junto com os tokens:

```swift
// REMOVER de extension View em DesignTokens.swift:
func retroGridOverlay(...) -> some View
func diagonalStripes(...) -> some View
func techCornerBorders(...) -> some View
func scanlineOverlay(...) -> some View
```

E as structs de pattern que os implementam:
- `RetroGridPattern`
- `DiagonalStripesPattern`
- `TechCornerBordersOverlay`
- `ScanlinePattern`

Buscar usos antes de remover: `grep -r "retroGridOverlay\|scanlineOverlay\|techCornerBorders\|diagonalStripes" --include="*.swift"`

---

## Tipografia — Avaliar

As fontes atuais (Orbitron, Rajdhani e Bungee) são tipografias de alta energia e tensão. Elas foram desenhadas para passar uma sensação de tecnologia,
  velocidade e impacto visual agressivo (muito comum em nichos de gaming ou cyberpunk). Para um posicionamento de bem-estar, calma e equilíbrio mental, elas
  geram um ruído visual que impede o usuário de "relaxar" dentro do app.

  Para uma nova identidade focada em constância e equilíbrio, o ideal é migrar para fontes Humanistas ou Geométricas Suaves. Abaixo, apresento as melhores opções
  para esse novo posicionamento:


  1. A Dupla Ideal (Equilíbrio e Modernidade)
   * Para Títulos: Plus Jakarta Sans
       * Por que: É uma fonte moderna, com formas abertas e curvas suaves. Ela passa uma sensação de "frescor" e limpeza sem ser fria ou industrial. É
         extremamente equilibrada e transmite confiança de forma gentil.
   * Para Corpo de Texto: Inter
       * Por que: É o padrão ouro para legibilidade digital. Ela é neutra, estável e "silenciosa". Não compete com o conteúdo, permitindo que o usuário processe
         as informações do treino sem esforço cognitivo desnecessário.

Recomendação técnica: Quando formos atualizar a identidade, recomendo adotar o par Plus Jakarta Sans (Títulos) + Inter (Corpo). É a combinação que melhor
  sustenta uma marca de Wellness moderna, garantindo que o app pareça uma ferramenta de cuidado, não um jogo de competição.

---

## Checklist

**`DesignTokens.swift` — Cores de marca:**
- [ ] `brandPrimary` → `#3B82F6`
- [ ] `brandSecondary` → `#60A5FA`
- [ ] `brandAccent` → `#FB7185` (coral — esforço/calorias)
- [ ] `background` → `#111111`
- [ ] `backgroundElevated` → `#1A1A1A`
- [ ] `surface` → `#1E1E1E`
- [ ] `surfaceElevated` → `#252525`
- [ ] `textSecondary` → `#94A3B8`
- [ ] `textTertiary` → `#64748B`
- [ ] `outline` → `#2A2A2A`
- [ ] `outlineVariant` → `#3A3A3A`
- [ ] `info` → unificar com `brandPrimary` (`#3B82F6`)

**`DesignTokens.swift` — Gradientes:**
- [ ] `gradientPrimary` → azul `#3B82F6` → `#1D4ED8`
- [ ] `gradientSecondary` → azul claro `#60A5FA` → `#3B82F6`
- [ ] Adicionar `gradientAccent` → coral `#FB7185` → `#F43F5E`
- [ ] Adicionar `gradientSurface` → subtil `#1A1A1A` → `#111111`
- [ ] `gradientStrength` → azul primário
- [ ] `gradientAerobic` → coral/rosa
- [ ] `gradientEndurance` → azul claro

**`DesignTokens.swift` — Remover legado:**
- [ ] Remover `neonCyan`, `neonMagenta`, `neonYellow`, `neonPurple`
- [ ] Remover `gridLine`, `gridAccent`, `scanLine`, `techBorder`
- [ ] Remover `glitchRed`, `glitchCyan`
- [ ] Remover `gradientRetroSunset`, `gradientSynthwave`, `gradientNeonGlow`
- [ ] Remover `retroGridOverlay`, `diagonalStripes`, `techCornerBorders`, `scanlineOverlay` de `extension View`
- [ ] Remover `RetroGridPattern`, `DiagonalStripesPattern`, `TechCornerBordersOverlay`, `ScanlinePattern`

**Verificação:**
- [ ] `grep -r "neonCyan\|neonMagenta\|neonPurple\|gridLine\|scanLine\|techBorder\|glitchRed\|gradientSynthwave" --include="*.swift"` → zero usos antes de remover
- [ ] Verificar no simulador: todas as telas em dark mode com a nova paleta
- [ ] Verificar contraste WCAG: texto branco sobre `#111111` ✅, `#94A3B8` sobre `#111111` deve atingir AA
- [ ] Verificar que `brandAccent` (`#FB7185`) NÃO é confundido com `error` (`#EF4444`) — tons suficientemente diferentes

## Notas

- `FitTodayColor.brandPrimary` é referenciado em muitos lugares via `fitGlowEffect(color:)` e `gradientPrimary` — a troca de hex é o único change necessário, sem renomear tokens
- `gradientPro` (dourado para usuários Pro) permanece: `#FFD700` → `#FFA500` — não muda
- `FitTodayColor.info` hoje é `#3B82F6` — ao unificar com `brandPrimary`, verificar se alguma view usa `info` esperando ser diferente do primary

## Contexto

O tema atual (`FitTodayColor` em `DesignTokens.swift`) é "Purple Futuristic / Retro-Synthwave" — cores neon agressivas, backgrounds roxo escuro, fontes Orbitron/Bungee com tracking alto, efeitos VHS e scanlines. Esse visual comunica adrenalina e high-performance, mas **não reflete o posicionamento atual do app**: constância e equilíbrio mental.

A nova paleta é mais calma, legível e acolhedora — ainda dark, mas sem o peso visual das neons.

---

| `brandPrimary` | `#7C3AED` (Roxo) | **`#3B82F6`** | Ação, botões, CTAs — confiança e clareza |
| `brandSecondary` | `#A78BFA` | **`#60A5FA`** | Variante mais clara do azul |
| `brandAccent` | `#5B21B6` | **`#FB7185`** | Coral/Rosa — esforço, calorias, destaque quente |
| `background` | `#0D0D14` (roxo escuro) | **`#111111`** | Preto neutro sem tinte de cor |
| `backgroundElevated` | `#1A1A28` | **`#1A1A1A`** | Neutro puro |
| `surface` | `#1E1E2E` | **`#1E1E1E`** | Cards e superfícies |
| `surfaceElevated` | `#24243A` | **`#252525`** | Cards elevados |
| `textSecondary` | `#A0A0B8` | **`#94A3B8`** | Cinza Lavanda — reduz fadiga ocular |
| `textTertiary` | `#64648C` | **`#64748B`** | Cinza slate para infos terciárias |
| `outline` | `#2A2A3C` | **`#2A2A2A`** | Bordas neutras |
| `outlineVariant` | `#3D3D52` | **`#3A3A3A`** | Bordas mais visíveis |

**Cores de status permanecem (não mudam):**
- `success`: `#22C55E`
- `warning`: `#F59E0B`
- `error`: `#EF4444`

```swift
static let gradientPrimary = LinearGradient(

)

    colors: [Color(hex: "#3B82F6"), Color(hex: "#1D4ED8")], ...
)

// Novo gradiente de acento (esforço/calorias)
static let gradientAccent = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#FB7185"), Color(hex: "#F43F5E")], ...
)

// Gradiente sutil para backgrounds de seção
static let gradientSurface = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#1A1A1A"), Color(hex: "#111111")], ...
)
```

### Gradientes de categoria de programa (ajustar para nova paleta)
- **Strength** → `#3B82F6` → `#1D4ED8` (azul primário, força/confiança)
- **Conditioning** → manter laranja `#F97316` → `#C2410C`
- **Aerobic** → `#FB7185` → `#F43F5E` (coral/rosa, esforço)
- **Endurance** → `#60A5FA` → `#3B82F6` (azul claro)
- **Wellness** → manter verde `#22C55E` → `#15803D`

---

Os tokens abaixo pertencem ao visual futurista e **não fazem sentido** na nova identidade de equilíbrio mental:

```swift
// REMOVER — neons agressivos
static let neonCyan = Color(hex: "#00E5FF")
static let neonMagenta = Color(hex: "#FF00E5")
static let neonYellow = Color(hex: "#FFEA00")
static let neonPurple = Color(hex: "#B026FF")

// REMOVER — elementos de grid tech

static let gridAccent = ...
static let scanLine = ...
static let techBorder = ...

// REMOVER — efeitos VHS/Glitch
static let glitchRed = ...
static let glitchCyan = ...

// REMOVER — gradientes retro
static let gradientRetroSunset = ...
static let gradientSynthwave = ...
static let gradientNeonGlow = ...
```

---

## Extensões de View — Remover efeitos retro

Os métodos de `View` abaixo também saem junto com os tokens:

```swift
// REMOVER de extension View em DesignTokens.swift:
func retroGridOverlay(...) -> some View
func diagonalStripes(...) -> some View
func techCornerBorders(...) -> some View
func scanlineOverlay(...) -> some View
```

E as structs de pattern que os implementam:

- `TechCornerBordersOverlay`
- `ScanlinePattern`

  sustenta uma marca de Wellness moderna, garantindo que o app pareça uma ferramenta de cuidado, não um jogo de competição.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/viniciuscarvalho/.claude/skills/feature-marker

# feature-marker

Automates feature development with a 5-phase workflow:

1. **Inputs Gate** - Validates `prd.md`, `techspec.md`, `tasks.md` exist; generates them via `~/.claude/commands/` if missing.
2. **Analysis & Planning** - Auto-installs product-manager skill if missing; reads docs, creates implementation plan.
3. **Implementation** - Executes tasks with progress tracking.
4. **Tests & Validation** - Runs platform-appropriate test suites and build validation.
   Auto-detects: Swift/Xcode (+ XcodeBuildMCP simulator), Node.js, Rust, Python, Go.
5. **Commit & PR** - Auto-installs enhanced commit command if missing; commits changes using professional workflow and creates PR (auto-detects git platform).

## Platform Support

feature-marker works with any tech stack:

- 🍎 **iOS/Swift** — `swift test` + SwiftLint + XcodeBuildMCP simulator validation
- 🟨 **Node.js/TypeScript** — auto-detects npm/yarn/pnpm/bun + Jest/Vitest
- 🦀 **Rust** — `cargo test` + `cargo clippy`
- 🐍 **Python** — `pytest` + ruff/flake8
- 🐹 **Go** — `go test` + `go vet`

iOS/Xcode projects get additional simulator validation via XcodeBuildMCP (optional, non-blocking).
The platform is auto-detected once at workflow start and cached — no configuration required.

## Usage

```
/feature-marker <feature-slug>
```

**Example**:
```
/feature-marker prd-user-authentication
```

### Interactive Mode

```
/feature-marker --interactive <feature-slug>
```

Opens a menu to select execution mode:
- **Full Workflow** - Default, generates missing files and executes all phases
- **Tasks Only** - Uses existing files, skips generation phase
- **Ralph Loop** - Autonomous continuous execution with ralph-wiggum
- **Spec-Driven** - Multi-agent review + worktree isolation via spec-workflow
- **Test Only** - Runs tests phase exclusively using platform-appropriate test guidance (Swift Testing for iOS, Jest/Vitest for Node.js, etc.)

Works both in terminal (TTY menu) and Claude CLI (AskUserQuestion prompt).

**Direct mode selection** (skip menu):
```
/feature-marker --mode full <feature-slug>
/feature-marker --mode tasks-only <feature-slug>
/feature-marker --mode ralph-loop <feature-slug>
/feature-marker --mode spec-driven <feature-slug>
/feature-marker --mode test-only <feature-slug>
```

## Prerequisites

### Commands

The following commands must be available in `~/.claude/commands/`:

- `create-prd.md` - Creates a new PRD from requirements discussion
- `generate-spec.md` - Generates technical specification from PRD
- `generate-tasks.md` - Breaks down feature spec into implementable tasks

### Templates

The commands above read templates from `~/.claude/docs/specs/` to generate structured documents.

Required templates:
- `~/.claude/docs/specs/prd-template.md` - Product Requirements Document template
- `~/.claude/docs/specs/techspec-template.md` - Technical Specification template
- `~/.claude/docs/specs/tasks-template.md` - Tasks breakdown template

**Template Format**: Templates should be markdown files with placeholders and structure that commands will use to generate feature-specific documents.

**Setup**: Ensure these templates exist before running feature-marker:
```bash
ls ~/.claude/docs/specs/
# Should show: prd-template.md, techspec-template.md, tasks-template.md
```

**Note**: If templates are missing, commands in `~/.claude/commands/` will fail to generate files.

### Project Structure

**Feature Documents** (generated in project):
```
./tasks/
└── prd-{feature-name}/
    ├── prd.md            ← Generated from ~/.claude/docs/specs/prd-template.md
    ├── techspec.md       ← Generated from ~/.claude/docs/specs/techspec-template.md
    ├── tasks.md          ← Generated from ~/.claude/docs/specs/tasks-template.md
    └── {num}_task.md     ← Individual task files (optional)
```

**State Directory** (checkpoint & progress):
```
.claude/feature-state/{feature-name}/
├── checkpoint.json
├── analysis.md
├── plan.md
├── progress.md
├── test-results.md
└── pr-url.txt
```

**User Configuration** (required setup):
```
~/.claude/
├── commands/           ← Commands that generate files
│   ├── create-prd.md
│   ├── generate-spec.md
│   └── generate-tasks.md
└── docs/
    └── specs/          ← Templates used by commands
        ├── prd-template.md
        ├── techspec-template.md
        └── tasks-template.md
```

## Behavior

When invoked, the skill:

1. **Validates inputs** - Checks if `./tasks/prd-{feature-slug}/` contains required files
   - If all files exist → Skips to step 3
   - If any file is missing → Proceeds to step 2
2. **Generates ONLY missing files** - Existing files are never overwritten:
   - Missing PRD → `/create-prd`
   - Missing Tech Spec → `/generate-spec {feature-slug}`
   - Missing Tasks → `/generate-tasks {feature-slug}`
3. **Auto-installs missing dependencies**:
   - **Phase 1**: Checks for `product-manager` skill
     - If missing: Installs via `npx skills add https://github.com/aj-geddes/claude-code-bmad-skills --skill product-manager`
     - If user already has it: Uses user's version
     - If installation fails: Continues without it (non-blocking)
   - **Phase 4**: Checks for `/commit` command
     - If missing: Copies from bundled `resources/commit.md` to `~/.claude/commands/commit.md`
     - If user already has it: Uses user's version
     - If installation fails: Falls back to standard commit workflow
4. **Executes 5-phase workflow** via the `feature-marker` agent
5. **Persists state** - Saves checkpoints after each phase/task for resume capability

**Important**: The workflow is smart about file detection and dependencies:
- ✅ Files/skills/commands exist → Uses them directly, no regeneration or reinstallation
- ⚠️ Missing → Installs/generates only what's needed
- 🔒 Never overwrites existing content
- 👤 **User's versions always have priority** over bundled/auto-installed versions

## Auto-Installed Dependencies

Feature-marker automatically installs missing dependencies to enhance the workflow:

### Product Manager Skill (Phase 1)

**What it does**: Provides advanced PRD analysis, requirements validation, and product management capabilities.

**Installation**:
- **Check**: Phase 1 checks for `~/.claude/skills/product-manager/SKILL.md`
- **Install**: If missing and `npx` available, runs:
  ```bash
  npx skills add https://github.com/aj-geddes/claude-code-bmad-skills --skill product-manager
  ```
- **Priority**: Uses user's existing skill if already installed
- **Fallback**: Continues without it if installation fails (non-blocking)

**Benefits**:
- Enhanced requirement analysis
- Better PRD validation
- Improved feature planning

### Enhanced Commit Command (Phase 4)

**What it does**: Professional commit workflow with validation, splitting, and conventional commit format.

**Installation**:
- **Check**: Phase 4 checks for `~/.claude/commands/commit.md`
- **Install**: If missing, copies from bundled `resources/commit.md` to `~/.claude/commands/commit.md`
- **Priority**: Uses user's existing command if already installed
- **Fallback**: Uses standard commit workflow if installation fails

**Features**:
- Pre-commit validation (lint, build, docs)
- Intelligent commit splitting
- Conventional commit format with emojis
- Smart file staging
- No Co-Authored-By footer (as per command design)

**Example Output**:
```bash
✨ feat: add user authentication system
🐛 fix: resolve memory leak in rendering process
📝 docs: update API documentation
♻️ refactor: simplify error handling logic
```

### Manual Installation

If auto-installation fails, you can install manually:

**Product Manager Skill**:
```bash
npx skills add https://github.com/aj-geddes/claude-code-bmad-skills --skill product-manager
```

**Commit Command**:
```bash
cp ~/.claude/skills/feature-marker/resources/commit.md ~/.claude/commands/commit.md
```

## Template Setup Guide

### Template Directory Structure

Commands in `~/.claude/commands/` read templates from a centralized location:

```
~/.claude/docs/specs/
├── prd-template.md          # Product Requirements Document template
├── techspec-template.md     # Technical Specification template
└── tasks-template.md        # Task breakdown template
```

### Why Templates in ~/.claude/docs/specs?

- **Centralized**: All projects share the same templates
- **User-controlled**: Users can customize their own templates
- **Portable**: Commands reference templates via standard path
- **Separation**: Templates are not in project repositories

### Template Content

Each template should be a markdown file with:
- Clear section structure
- Placeholder text or variables
- Examples and formatting guidelines

Commands read these templates and populate them with feature-specific content.

### Setup Verification

To verify your setup is complete:

```bash
# Check templates exist
ls -l ~/.claude/docs/specs/

# Check commands exist
ls -l ~/.claude/commands/

# Test feature-marker
/feature-marker --interactive prd-test-feature
```

If templates are missing, create them in `~/.claude/docs/specs/` before running feature-marker.

## Plan Mode Integration

When the user has used Claude's built-in plan mode before invoking feature-marker, the agent automatically:

1. **Detects the most recent plan** from `~/.claude/plans/` (sorted by modification time)
2. **Reads project conventions** from `./CLAUDE.md` at the project root (if present)
3. **Uses both as rich context** to enhance PRD generation and reduce redundant clarification questions

This is automatic and requires no additional flags or options. If no plan or CLAUDE.md exists, the workflow proceeds normally.

**Recommended flow**:
```
1. Use Claude plan mode to explore the codebase and think through the feature
2. Exit plan mode
3. Run /feature-marker --interactive <feature-slug>
4. Select "Full Workflow"
5. The plan content automatically enriches PRD generation
```

## Checkpoint & Resume

If interrupted (Ctrl+C, session crash, etc.), re-invoke with the same feature slug to resume:

```
/feature-marker prd-user-authentication
```

The skill will:
- Detect existing checkpoint
- Show current progress (phase, task index)
- Ask if you want to resume or start fresh

## Platform Detection

In Phase 4, the skill auto-detects your git platform and selects the appropriate PR skill:

| Platform | Detection | PR Skill |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| GitHub | `github.com` in remote URL | `checking-pr` |
| Azure DevOps | `dev.azure.com` in remote URL | `azure-pr` |
| GitLab | `gitlab.com` in remote URL | `checking-pr` |
| Bitbucket | `bitbucket.org` in remote URL | `checking-pr` |
| Other | (fallback) | `checking-pr` |

## Configuration

Override default behavior with `.feature-marker.json` in your repository root:

```json
{
  "pr_skill": "custom-pr-skill",
  "skip_pr": false,
  "test_command": "npm run test:ci",
  "docs_path": "./tasks",
  "state_path": ".claude/feature-state"
}
```

## Error Handling

| Scenario | Behavior |
|----------|----------|
| Missing files | Auto-generate via commands |
| No git repo | Fail early with helpful message |
| No tests | Skip Phase 3 with warning |
| Test failures | Report issues, allow fix, offer retry |
| Unknown platform | Fallback to `checking-pr` |
| PR skill unavailable | Commit only, log manual instructions |

## Example Sessions

### Example 1: All Files Exist (No Generation Needed)
```
> /feature-marker prd-user-authentication

Checking for existing checkpoint...
No checkpoint found. Starting new workflow.
```

   324→// MARK: - Color Extension for Hex
   325→
   326→extension Color {
   327→    init(hex: String) {
   328→        let hex = hex.trimmingCharacters(in: CharacterSet.alphanumerics.inverted)
   329→        var int: UInt64 = 0
   330→        Scanner(string: hex).scanHexInt64(&int)
   331→        let a, r, g, b: UInt64
   332→        switch hex.count {
   333→        case 3: // RGB (12-bit)
   334→            (a, r, g, b) = (255, (int >> 8) * 17, (int >> 4 & 0xF) * 17, (int & 0xF) * 17)
   335→        case 6: // RGB (24-bit)
   336→            (a, r, g, b) = (255, int >> 16, int >> 8 & 0xFF, int & 0xFF)
   337→        case 8: // ARGB (32-bit)
   338→            (a, r, g, b) = (int >> 24, int >> 16 & 0xFF, int >> 8 & 0xFF, int & 0xFF)
   339→        default:
   340→            (a, r, g, b) = (255, 0, 0, 0)
   341→        }
   342→        self.init(
   343→            .sRGB,
   344→            red: Double(r) / 255,
   345→            green: Double(g) / 255,
   346→            blue: Double(b) / 255,
   347→            opacity: Double(a) / 255
   348→        )
   349→    }
   350→}

   352→// MARK: - Retro Pattern Views
   353→
   354→struct RetroGridPattern: View {
   355→    let lineColor: Color
   356→    let spacing: CGFloat
   357→
   358→    var body: some View {
   359→        GeometryReader { geometry in
   360→            Path { path in
   361→                // Vertical lines
   362→                let cols = Int(geometry.size.width / spacing)
   363→                for i in 0...cols {
   364→                    let x = CGFloat(i) * spacing
   365→                    path.move(to: CGPoint(x: x, y: 0))
   366→                    path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: x, y: geometry.size.height))
   367→                }
   368→
   369→                // Horizontal lines
   370→                let rows = Int(geometry.size.height / spacing)
   371→                for i in 0...rows {
   372→                    let y = CGFloat(i) * spacing
   373→                    path.move(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: y))
   374→                    path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: y))
   375→                }
   376→            }
   377→            .stroke(lineColor, lineWidth: 1)
   378→        }
   379→        .allowsHitTesting(false)
   380→        .accessibilityHidden(true)
   381→    }
   382→}
   383→
   384→struct DiagonalStripesPattern: View {
   385→    let color: Color
   386→    let spacing: CGFloat
   387→    let opacity: Double
   388→
   389→    var body: some View {
   390→        GeometryReader { geometry in
   391→            Path { path in
   392→                let diagonal = sqrt(pow(geometry.size.width, 2) + pow(geometry.size.height, 2))
   393→                let stripes = Int(diagonal / spacing)
   394→
   395→                for i in 0...stripes {
   396→                    let offset = CGFloat(i) * spacing
   397→                    path.move(to: CGPoint(x: -geometry.size.height + offset, y: 0))
   398→                    path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: offset, y: geometry.size.height))
   399→                }
   400→            }
   401→            .stroke(color.opacity(opacity), lineWidth: 2)
   402→        }
   403→        .rotationEffect(.degrees(45))
   404→        .allowsHitTesting(false)
   405→        .accessibilityHidden(true)
   406→    }
   407→}
   408→
   409→struct TechCornerBordersOverlay: View {

   413→

   416→            Path { path in
   417→                // Top-left
   418→                path.move(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: length))
   419→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: 0))
   420→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: length, y: 0))
   421→
   422→                // Top-right
   423→                path.move(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width - length, y: 0))
   424→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: 0))
   425→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: length))
   426→
   427→                // Bottom-left
   428→                path.move(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: geometry.size.height - length))
   429→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: geometry.size.height))
   430→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: length, y: geometry.size.height))
   431→
   432→                // Bottom-right
   433→                path.move(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width - length, y: geometry.size.height))
   434→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: geometry.size.height))
   435→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: geometry.size.height - length))
   436→            }
   437→            .stroke(color, lineWidth: thickness)
   438→        }
   439→        .allowsHitTesting(false)
   440→        .accessibilityHidden(true)
   441→    }
   442→}

   457→            }

# Product Requirements Document (PRD)

**Project Name:** Nova Identidade Visual — Constancia & Equilibrio Mental
**Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-03-05
**Author:** Claude Code
**Status:** Draft

---

## Executive Summary

**Problem Statement:**
O tema atual do FitToday ("Purple Futuristic / Retro-Synthwave") comunica adrenalina e high-performance com cores neon agressivas, backgrounds roxo escuro e fontes Orbitron/Bungee. Esse visual nao reflete o posicionamento atual do app: constancia e equilibrio mental.

**Proposed Solution:**
Migrar a paleta de cores de roxo neon para azul calmo, substituir fontes retro por tipografia humanista (Plus Jakarta Sans + Inter), e remover todos os efeitos visuais legado (grid, scanline, glitch, neon).

**Business Value:**
- Alinhamento visual com o posicionamento de bem-estar e constancia
- Melhor legibilidade e menor fadiga ocular em dark mode
- App mais acolhedor para novos usuarios

**Success Metrics:**
- Zero tokens legado (neon/grid/scanline/glitch) no codebase
- Todas as cores de marca atualizadas para nova paleta
- Tipografia migrada para Plus Jakarta Sans + Inter
- Build limpo sem erros

---

## Functional Requirements

### FR-001: Atualizar Paleta de Cores de Marca [MUST]

**Description:**
Substituir todas as cores de marca em `FitTodayColor` de roxo/purple para azul calmo e coral.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `brandPrimary` = `#3B82F6` (azul)
- `brandSecondary` = `#60A5FA` (azul claro)
- `brandAccent` = `#FB7185` (coral/rosa)
- `background` = `#111111` (preto neutro)
- `backgroundElevated` = `#1A1A1A`
- `surface` = `#1E1E1E`
- `surfaceElevated` = `#252525`
- `textSecondary` = `#94A3B8` (cinza lavanda)
- `textTertiary` = `#64748B` (cinza slate)
- `outline` = `#2A2A2A`
- `outlineVariant` = `#3A3A3A`
- `info` unificado com `brandPrimary` (`#3B82F6`)

---

### FR-002: Atualizar Gradientes [MUST]

**Description:**
Substituir gradientes de roxo para nova paleta azul/coral.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `gradientPrimary` = `#3B82F6` -> `#1D4ED8`
- `gradientSecondary` = `#60A5FA` -> `#3B82F6`
- Novo `gradientAccent` = `#FB7185` -> `#F43F5E`
- Novo `gradientSurface` = `#1A1A1A` -> `#111111`
- `gradientStrength` = azul primario `#3B82F6` -> `#1D4ED8`
- `gradientAerobic` = coral `#FB7185` -> `#F43F5E`
- `gradientEndurance` = azul claro `#60A5FA` -> `#3B82F6`
- `gradientConditioning` permanece laranja (nao muda)
- `gradientWellness` permanece verde (nao muda)
- `gradientPro` permanece dourado (nao muda)

---

### FR-003: Remover Tokens e Efeitos Legado [MUST]

**Description:**
Remover todos os tokens, views e patterns do tema retro-futurista.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Remover cores: `neonCyan`, `neonMagenta`, `neonYellow`, `neonPurple`
- Remover cores: `gridLine`, `gridAccent`, `scanLine`, `techBorder`
- Remover cores: `glitchRed`, `glitchCyan`
- Remover gradientes: `gradientRetroSunset`, `gradientSynthwave`, `gradientNeonGlow`
- Remover extension View: `retroGridOverlay`, `diagonalStripes`, `techCornerBorders`, `scanlineOverlay`
- Remover structs: `RetroGridPattern`, `DiagonalStripesPattern`, `TechCornerBordersOverlay`, `ScanlinePattern`
- Remover todos os usos desses tokens/efeitos em views do app

---

### FR-004: Migrar Tipografia [MUST]

**Description:**
Substituir fontes retro (Orbitron, Rajdhani, Bungee) por fontes humanistas (Plus Jakarta Sans para titulos, Inter para corpo).

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `FitTodayFont` usa Plus Jakarta Sans (titulos) e Inter (corpo)
- Remover referencia a Orbitron, Rajdhani, Bungee
- Reduzir tracking para valores menores (mais calmo)
- 565 usos em 90 arquivos atualizados
- Fontes .ttf/.otf adicionadas ao bundle e Info.plist

---

## Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-001: Contraste WCAG [MUST]

**Description:**
Garantir contraste adequado para acessibilidade.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Texto branco sobre `#111111` atinge WCAG AA
- `#94A3B8` (textSecondary) sobre `#111111` atinge WCAG AA
- `brandAccent` (`#FB7185`) visualmente distinto de `error` (`#EF4444`)

### NFR-002: Zero Breaking Changes na API [MUST]

**Description:**
Nenhum token deve ser renomeado — apenas valores hex sao alterados. Tokens removidos devem primeiro ter zero usos confirmados.

---

## Out of Scope

1. Light mode — continua apenas dark mode
2. Redesign de layouts ou componentes — apenas cores e fontes mudam
3. Animacoes ou transicoes novas
4. Mudanca em cores de status (success, warning, error)
5. Mudanca em `gradientPro` (dourado para usuarios Pro)

---

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| Fontes customizadas nao carregam em runtime | Alto | Medio | Testar em simulador imediatamente apos adicionar; fallback para system fonts |
| Tokens legado usados em views nao detectadas | Medio | Baixo | grep exaustivo antes de remover |
| Conflito visual brandAccent vs error | Medio | Baixo | Cores suficientemente diferentes (#FB7185 vs #EF4444) |
| 90 arquivos precisam mudar tipografia | Alto | Alto | Mudanca centralizada em FitTodayFont minimiza impacto |

---

## References

- GitHub Issue: https://github.com/Viniciuscarvalho/FitToday/issues/65
- Arquivo principal: `FitToday/Presentation/DesignSystem/DesignTokens.swift`

# Technical Specification

**Project Name:** Nova Identidade Visual — Constancia & Equilibrio Mental
**Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-03-05
**Author:** Claude Code
**Status:** Draft

---

## Overview

### Problem Statement
O design system atual usa tema "Purple Futuristic / Retro-Synthwave" com cores neon, fontes retro e efeitos visuais agressivos. A mudanca requer atualizar `DesignTokens.swift` (centro do design system) e propagar para ~90 arquivos que usam tipografia customizada.

### Goals
- Atualizar paleta de cores (hex swap centralizado)
- Remover tokens/efeitos legado retro
- Migrar tipografia de Orbitron/Rajdhani/Bungee para Plus Jakarta Sans/Inter
- Adicionar novos gradientes (accent, surface)

---

## Scope

### In Scope
- `DesignTokens.swift` — cores, gradientes, fontes, extensions, patterns
- Font bundle — adicionar Plus Jakarta Sans e Inter, remover Orbitron/Rajdhani/Bungee
- Limpeza de usos de tokens legado em views

### Out of Scope
- Mudancas de layout ou componentes
- Light mode
- Animacoes
- Cores de status (success/warning/error)
- gradientPro

---

## Technical Approach

### Architecture Overview

O design system e centralizado em `FitTodayColor`, `FitTodayFont`, `FitTodayTypography` dentro de `DesignTokens.swift`. Todas as views referenciam esses enums. A mudanca e primariamente um swap de valores — nenhuma interface publica muda de nome.

### Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Presentation/DesignSystem/DesignTokens.swift` | Cores, gradientes, fontes, remover legacy |
| `Info.plist` (or build settings) | Registrar novas fontes, remover antigas |
| `*.swift` (90 arquivos) | Apenas se usam tokens legado diretamente |

### Component 1: Color Palette Swap

Mudanca puramente de valores hex. Nenhum token e renomeado.

```swift
// BEFORE
static let brandPrimary = Color(hex: "#7C3AED")
// AFTER
static let brandPrimary = Color(hex: "#3B82F6")
```

**Full mapping:**
| Token | Old Hex | New Hex |
|-------|---------|---------|
| brandPrimary | #7C3AED | #3B82F6 |
| brandSecondary | #A78BFA | #60A5FA |
| brandAccent | #5B21B6 | #FB7185 |
| background | #0D0D14 | #111111 |
| backgroundElevated | #1A1A28 | #1A1A1A |
| surface | #1E1E2E | #1E1E1E |
| surfaceElevated | #24243A | #252525 |
| textSecondary | #A0A0B8 | #94A3B8 |
| textTertiary | #64648C | #64748B |
| outline | #2A2A3C | #2A2A2A |
| outlineVariant | #3D3D52 | #3A3A3A |

### Component 2: Gradient Updates

```swift
// Primary: azul calmo
static let gradientPrimary = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#3B82F6"), Color(hex: "#1D4ED8")], ...
)

// Accent: coral (novo)
static let gradientAccent = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#FB7185"), Color(hex: "#F43F5E")], ...
)

// Surface: sutil (novo)
static let gradientSurface = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#1A1A1A"), Color(hex: "#111111")], ...
)
```

Category gradients:
| Category | Old | New |
|----------|-----|-----|
| Strength | #7C3AED->#5B21B6 | #3B82F6->#1D4ED8 |
| Aerobic | #EC4899->#BE185D | #FB7185->#F43F5E |
| Endurance | #3B82F6->#1D4ED8 | #60A5FA->#3B82F6 |
| Conditioning | no change | no change |
| Wellness | no change | no change |

### Component 3: Legacy Removal

Remove ~110 lines from DesignTokens.swift:
- Lines 103-138: neon colors, grid/tech, glitch, retro gradients
- Lines 293-322: retroGridOverlay, diagonalStripes, techCornerBorders, scanlineOverlay view extensions
- Lines 352-463: RetroGridPattern, DiagonalStripesPattern, TechCornerBordersOverlay, ScanlinePattern structs

**Pre-removal verification:**
```bash
grep -r "neonCyan\|neonMagenta\|neonPurple\|gridLine\|scanLine\|techBorder\|glitchRed\|gradientSynthwave" --include="*.swift" | grep -v DesignTokens.swift
```
Must return zero results.

### Component 4: Typography Migration

**New fonts:**
- Plus Jakarta Sans: Bold, SemiBold, Medium (titulos, headings)
- Inter: Regular, Medium, SemiBold (corpo, captions)

**Font registration:**
Add .ttf/.otf files to Xcode target and register in Info.plist under `UIAppFonts`.

**FitTodayFont changes:**
```swift
enum FitTodayFont {
    // Font names
    static let jakartaBold = "PlusJakartaSans-Bold"
    static let jakartaSemiBold = "PlusJakartaSans-SemiBold"
    static let jakartaMedium = "PlusJakartaSans-Medium"
    static let interRegular = "Inter-Regular"
    static let interMedium = "Inter-Medium"
    static let interSemiBold = "Inter-SemiBold"

    static func display(size: CGFloat, weight: DisplayWeight = .bold) -> Font {
        switch weight {
        case .bold: return .custom(jakartaBold, size: size)
        case .extraBold: return .custom(jakartaBold, size: size)
        case .black: return .custom(jakartaBold, size: size)
        }
    }

    static func ui(size: CGFloat, weight: UIWeight = .medium) -> Font {
        switch weight {
        case .medium: return .custom(interMedium, size: size)
        case .semiBold: return .custom(interSemiBold, size: size)
        case .bold: return .custom(interSemiBold, size: size)
        }
    }

    static func accent(size: CGFloat) -> Font {
        .custom(jakartaSemiBold, size: size)
    }
}
```

**Tracking reduction in FitTodayTypography:**
| Level | Old Tracking | New Tracking |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| largeTitle | 1.5 | 0.3 |
| title | 1.2 | 0.2 |
| title2 | 1.0 | 0.2 |
| heading | 0.8 | 0.1 |
| subheading | 0.5 | 0 |
| body | 0.3 | 0 |
| caption | 0.5 | 0.2 |
| badge | 0.8 | 0.5 |

### Component 5: Comment/Header Updates

- Update file header comment from "Purple Futuristic" to "Calm Blue / Wellness"
- Update MARK comments to reflect new theme

---

## Testing Strategy

### Verification Steps
1. `grep` para tokens legado → zero hits fora de DesignTokens.swift
2. Build limpo no simulador
3. Visual check das telas principais: Home, Workouts, Programs, Profile
4. Contraste WCAG: texto branco sobre #111111, #94A3B8 sobre #111111
5. Fontes carregam corretamente (nao fallback para system)

### Build Validation
- `mcp__xcodebuildmcp__build_sim_name_proj` deve passar sem erros
- Nenhum warning de font not found em runtime

---

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| Font files nao incluidas no bundle | Alto | Medio | Verificar Target Membership apos adicionar |
| Token legado usado em view nao detectada | Medio | Baixo | grep exaustivo com pattern completo |
| FitTodayFont.display/ui API muda | Alto | Baixo | Manter mesma API, so trocar nomes internos |

# Technical Specification

**Project Name:** Nova Identidade Visual — Constancia & Equilibrio Mental
**Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-03-05
**Author:** Claude Code
**Status:** Draft

---

## Overview

### Problem Statement
O design system atual usa tema "Purple Futuristic / Retro-Synthwave" com cores neon, fontes retro e efeitos visuais agressivos. A mudanca requer atualizar `DesignTokens.swift` (centro do design system) e propagar para ~90 arquivos que usam tipografia customizada.

### Goals
- Atualizar paleta de cores (hex swap centralizado)
- Remover tokens/efeitos legado retro
- Migrar tipografia de Orbitron/Rajdhani/Bungee para Plus Jakarta Sans/Inter
- Adicionar novos gradientes (accent, surface)

---

## Scope

### In Scope
- `DesignTokens.swift` — cores, gradientes, fontes, extensions, patterns
- Font bundle — adicionar Plus Jakarta Sans e Inter, remover Orbitron/Rajdhani/Bungee
- Limpeza de usos de tokens legado em views

### Out of Scope
- Mudancas de layout ou componentes
- Light mode
- Animacoes
- Cores de status (success/warning/error)
- gradientPro

---

## Technical Approach

### Architecture Overview

O design system e centralizado em `FitTodayColor`, `FitTodayFont`, `FitTodayTypography` dentro de `DesignTokens.swift`. Todas as views referenciam esses enums. A mudanca e primariamente um swap de valores — nenhuma interface publica muda de nome.

### Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Presentation/DesignSystem/DesignTokens.swift` | Cores, gradientes, fontes, remover legacy |
| `Info.plist` (or build settings) | Registrar novas fontes, remover antigas |
| `*.swift` (90 arquivos) | Apenas se usam tokens legado diretamente |

### Component 1: Color Palette Swap

Mudanca puramente de valores hex. Nenhum token e renomeado.

```swift
// BEFORE
static let brandPrimary = Color(hex: "#7C3AED")
// AFTER
static let brandPrimary = Color(hex: "#3B82F6")
```

**Full mapping:**
| Token | Old Hex | New Hex |
|-------|---------|---------|
| brandPrimary | #7C3AED | #3B82F6 |
| brandSecondary | #A78BFA | #60A5FA |
| brandAccent | #5B21B6 | #FB7185 |
| background | #0D0D14 | #111111 |
| backgroundElevated | #1A1A28 | #1A1A1A |
| surface | #1E1E2E | #1E1E1E |
| surfaceElevated | #24243A | #252525 |
| textSecondary | #A0A0B8 | #94A3B8 |
| textTertiary | #64648C | #64748B |
| outline | #2A2A3C | #2A2A2A |
| outlineVariant | #3D3D52 | #3A3A3A |

### Component 2: Gradient Updates

```swift
// Primary: azul calmo
static let gradientPrimary = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#3B82F6"), Color(hex: "#1D4ED8")], ...
)

// Accent: coral (novo)
static let gradientAccent = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#FB7185"), Color(hex: "#F43F5E")], ...
)

// Surface: sutil (novo)
static let gradientSurface = LinearGradient(
    colors: [Color(hex: "#1A1A1A"), Color(hex: "#111111")], ...
)
```

Category gradients:
| Category | Old | New |
|----------|-----|-----|
| Strength | #7C3AED->#5B21B6 | #3B82F6->#1D4ED8 |
| Aerobic | #EC4899->#BE185D | #FB7185->#F43F5E |
| Endurance | #3B82F6->#1D4ED8 | #60A5FA->#3B82F6 |
| Conditioning | no change | no change |
| Wellness | no change | no change |

### Component 3: Legacy Removal

Remove ~110 lines from DesignTokens.swift:
- Lines 103-138: neon colors, grid/tech, glitch, retro gradients
- Lines 293-322: retroGridOverlay, diagonalStripes, techCornerBorders, scanlineOverlay view extensions
- Lines 352-463: RetroGridPattern, DiagonalStripesPattern, TechCornerBordersOverlay, ScanlinePattern structs

**Pre-removal verification:**
```bash
grep -r "neonCyan\|neonMagenta\|neonPurple\|gridLine\|scanLine\|techBorder\|glitchRed\|gradientSynthwave" --include="*.swift" | grep -v DesignTokens.swift
```
Must return zero results.

### Component 4: Typography Migration

**New fonts:**
- Plus Jakarta Sans: Bold, SemiBold, Medium (titulos, headings)
- Inter: Regular, Medium, SemiBold (corpo, captions)

**Font registration:**
Add .ttf/.otf files to Xcode target and register in Info.plist under `UIAppFonts`.

**FitTodayFont changes:**
```swift
enum FitTodayFont {
    // Font names
    static let jakartaBold = "PlusJakartaSans-Bold"
    static let jakartaSemiBold = "PlusJakartaSans-SemiBold"
    static let jakartaMedium = "PlusJakartaSans-Medium"
    static let interRegular = "Inter-Regular"
    static let interMedium = "Inter-Medium"
    static let interSemiBold = "Inter-SemiBold"

    static func display(size: CGFloat, weight: DisplayWeight = .bold) -> Font {
        switch weight {
        case .bold: return .custom(jakartaBold, size: size)
        case .extraBold: return .custom(jakartaBold, size: size)
        case .black: return .custom(jakartaBold, size: size)
        }
    }

    static func ui(size: CGFloat, weight: UIWeight = .medium) -> Font {
        switch weight {
        case .medium: return .custom(interMedium, size: size)
        case .semiBold: return .custom(interSemiBold, size: size)
        case .bold: return .custom(interSemiBold, size: size)
        }
    }

    static func accent(size: CGFloat) -> Font {
        .custom(jakartaSemiBold, size: size)
    }
}
```

**Tracking reduction in FitTodayTypography:**
| Level | Old Tracking | New Tracking |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| largeTitle | 1.5 | 0.3 |
| title | 1.2 | 0.2 |
| title2 | 1.0 | 0.2 |
| heading | 0.8 | 0.1 |
| subheading | 0.5 | 0 |
| body | 0.3 | 0 |
| caption | 0.5 | 0.2 |
| badge | 0.8 | 0.5 |

### Component 5: Comment/Header Updates

- Update file header comment from "Purple Futuristic" to "Calm Blue / Wellness"
- Update MARK comments to reflect new theme

---

## Testing Strategy

### Verification Steps
1. `grep` para tokens legado → zero hits fora de DesignTokens.swift
2. Build limpo no simulador
3. Visual check das telas principais: Home, Workouts, Programs, Profile
4. Contraste WCAG: texto branco sobre #111111, #94A3B8 sobre #111111
5. Fontes carregam corretamente (nao fallback para system)

### Build Validation
- `mcp__xcodebuildmcp__build_sim_name_proj` deve passar sem erros
- Nenhum warning de font not found em runtime

---

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| Font files nao incluidas no bundle | Alto | Medio | Verificar Target Membership apos adicionar |
| Token legado usado em view nao detectada | Medio | Baixo | grep exaustivo com pattern completo |
| FitTodayFont.display/ui API muda | Alto | Baixo | Manter mesma API, so trocar nomes internos |

---

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Todas as cores de marca atualizadas para nova paleta
- [ ] Gradientes primarios e de categoria atualizados
- [ ] Todos os tokens/efeitos legado removidos
- [ ] Fontes Plus Jakarta Sans e Inter funcionando
- [ ] Tracking reduzido em FitTodayTypography
- [ ] Build limpo sem erros
- [ ] Zero referencias a tokens removidos

There are many usages of the legacy tokens outside DesignTokens.swift. I need to clean them all up. This is a significant amount of work. Let me use agents to do this in parallel — one to clean up the views and another to clean up the design system files.

Actually, let me think about the approach. The issue says to remove these tokens and effects. The views that use them need to be cleaned up by either:
1. Removing the retro effects entirely (`.retroGridOverlay`, `.scanlineOverlay`, `.techCornerBorders`, `.diagonalStripes`)

Now I'm mapping out the color replacements: neon cyan becomes the primary brand color, magenta shifts to the accent, yellow aligns with the warning state, and purple takes on the secondary brand role. For the retro effects, I'll be removing the grid overlay, scanlines, corner borders, and diagonal stripe modifiers entirely.

Given the scope of changes across multiple files, I think using subagents to parallelize the replacements by file groups makes more sense than doing this manually one by one.

input.description: Clean legacy retro effects from design system files
input.prompt:
You need to clean up legacy retro/futuristic visual effects from the FitToday design system files. The app is migrating from a "Purple Futuristic / Retro-Synthwave" theme to a calm blue wellness theme.

**Color replacements (across ALL files you touch):**
- `FitTodayColor.neonCyan` → `FitTodayColor.brandPrimary`
- `FitTodayColor.neonMagenta` → `FitTodayColor.brandAccent`
- `FitTodayColor.neonYellow` → `FitTodayColor.warning`
- `FitTodayColor.neonPurple` → `FitTodayColor.brandSecondary`
- `FitTodayColor.gridLine` → `FitTodayColor.outline`
- `FitTodayColor.techBorder` → `FitTodayColor.outline`
- `FitTodayColor.glitchRed` → `FitTodayColor.error`

**Retro effects to REMOVE entirely (just delete the modifier call):**
- `.retroGridOverlay(...)` — delete the entire line
- `.scanlineOverlay(...)` — delete the entire line
- `.diagonalStripes(...)` — delete the entire line
- `.techCornerBorders(...)` — replace with `.fitCardBorder()` where it makes sense for a card, otherwise delete

**Direct struct usage to replace:**
- `RetroGridPattern(lineColor: ..., spacing: ...)` — replace with `Color.clear` or just remove the overlay

**Files to modify (design system):**
1. `FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/DesignSystem/ButtonStyles.swift`
2. `FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/DesignSystem/CardsAndBadges.swift`
3. `FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/DesignSystem/FitnessIcons.swift`
4. `FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/DesignSystem/ExercisePlaceholderView.swift`
5. `FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/DesignSystem/RestTimerView.swift`

Read each file first, then make the changes. Be thorough — remove ALL retro effects and replace ALL neon colors. Keep the code clean and properly formatted.

   300→// MARK: - Color Extension for Hex
   301→
   302→extension Color {
   303→    init(hex: String) {
   304→        let hex = hex.trimmingCharacters(in: CharacterSet.alphanumerics.inverted)
   305→        var int: UInt64 = 0
   306→        Scanner(string: hex).scanHexInt64(&int)
   307→        let a, r, g, b: UInt64
   308→        switch hex.count {
   309→        case 3: // RGB (12-bit)
   310→            (a, r, g, b) = (255, (int >> 8) * 17, (int >> 4 & 0xF) * 17, (int & 0xF) * 17)
   311→        case 6: // RGB (24-bit)
   312→            (a, r, g, b) = (255, int >> 16, int >> 8 & 0xFF, int & 0xFF)
   313→        case 8: // ARGB (32-bit)
   314→            (a, r, g, b) = (int >> 24, int >> 16 & 0xFF, int >> 8 & 0xFF, int & 0xFF)
   315→        default:
   316→            (a, r, g, b) = (255, 0, 0, 0)
   317→        }
   318→        self.init(
   319→            .sRGB,
   320→            red: Double(r) / 255,
   321→            green: Double(g) / 255,
   322→            blue: Double(b) / 255,
   323→            opacity: Double(a) / 255
   324→        )
   325→    }
   326→}
   327→
   328→// MARK: - Retro Pattern Views
   329→
   330→struct RetroGridPattern: View {
   331→    let lineColor: Color
   332→    let spacing: CGFloat
   333→
   334→    var body: some View {
   335→        GeometryReader { geometry in
   336→            Path { path in
   337→                // Vertical lines
   338→                let cols = Int(geometry.size.width / spacing)
   339→                for i in 0...cols {
   340→                    let x = CGFloat(i) * spacing
   341→                    path.move(to: CGPoint(x: x, y: 0))
   342→                    path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: x, y: geometry.size.height))
   343→                }
   344→
   345→                // Horizontal lines
   346→                let rows = Int(geometry.size.height / spacing)
   347→                for i in 0...rows {
   348→                    let y = CGFloat(i) * spacing
   349→                    path.move(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: y))
   350→                    path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: y))
   351→                }
   352→            }
   353→            .stroke(lineColor, lineWidth: 1)
   354→        }
   355→        .allowsHitTesting(false)
   356→        .accessibilityHidden(true)
   357→    }
   358→}
   359→
   360→struct DiagonalStripesPattern: View {
   361→    let color: Color
   362→    let spacing: CGFloat
   363→    let opacity: Double
   364→
   365→    var body: some View {
   366→        GeometryReader { geometry in
   367→            Path { path in
   368→                let diagonal = sqrt(pow(geometry.size.width, 2) + pow(geometry.size.height, 2))
   369→                let stripes = Int(diagonal / spacing)
   370→
   371→                for i in 0...stripes {
   372→                    let offset = CGFloat(i) * spacing
   373→                    path.move(to: CGPoint(x: -geometry.size.height + offset, y: 0))
   374→                    path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: offset, y: geometry.size.height))
   375→                }
   376→            }
   377→            .stroke(color.opacity(opacity), lineWidth: 2)
   378→        }
   379→        .rotationEffect(.degrees(45))
   380→        .allowsHitTesting(false)
   381→        .accessibilityHidden(true)
   382→    }
   383→}
   384→
   385→struct TechCornerBordersOverlay: View {
   386→    let color: Color
   387→    let length: CGFloat
   388→    let thickness: CGFloat
   389→
   390→    var body: some View {
   391→        GeometryReader { geometry in
   392→            Path { path in
   393→                // Top-left
   394→                path.move(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: length))
   395→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: 0))
   396→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: length, y: 0))
   397→
   398→                // Top-right
   399→                path.move(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width - length, y: 0))
   400→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: 0))
   401→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: length))
   402→
   403→                // Bottom-left
   404→                path.move(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: geometry.size.height - length))
   405→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: geometry.size.height))
   406→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: length, y: geometry.size.height))
   407→
   408→                // Bottom-right
   409→                path.move(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width - length, y: geometry.size.height))
   410→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: geometry.size.height))
   411→                path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: geometry.size.width, y: geometry.size.height - length))
   412→            }

   138→
   139→// MARK: - Font System (Retro-Futuristic)
   140→
   141→enum FitTodayFont {
   142→    // Font names
   143→    static let orbitronBold = "Orbitron-Bold"
   144→    static let orbitronExtraBold = "Orbitron-ExtraBold"
   145→    static let orbitronBlack = "Orbitron-Black"
   146→    static let rajdhaniBold = "Rajdhani-Bold"
   147→    static let rajdhaniSemiBold = "Rajdhani-SemiBold"
   148→    static let rajdhaniMedium = "Rajdhani-Medium"
   149→    static let bungeeRegular = "Bungee-Regular"
   150→
   151→    // Display fonts (Orbitron)
   152→    static func display(size: CGFloat, weight: DisplayWeight = .bold) -> Font {
   153→        switch weight {
   154→        case .bold: return .custom(orbitronBold, size: size)
   155→        case .extraBold: return .custom(orbitronExtraBold, size: size)
   156→        case .black: return .custom(orbitronBlack, size: size)
   157→        }
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    // UI fonts (Rajdhani)
   161→    static func ui(size: CGFloat, weight: UIWeight = .medium) -> Font {
   162→        switch weight {
   163→        case .medium: return .custom(rajdhaniMedium, size: size)
   164→        case .semiBold: return .custom(rajdhaniSemiBold, size: size)
   165→        case .bold: return .custom(rajdhaniBold, size: size)
   166→        }
   167→    }
   168→
   169→    // Accent font (Bungee)
   170→    static func accent(size: CGFloat) -> Font {
   171→        .custom(bungeeRegular, size: size)
   172→    }
   173→
   174→    enum DisplayWeight {
   175→        case bold, extraBold, black
   176→    }
   177→
   178→    enum UIWeight {
   179→        case medium, semiBold, bold
   180→    }
   181→}
   182→
   183→// MARK: - Typography (Retro-Futuristic)
   184→
   185→enum FitTodayTypography {
   186→    static func largeTitle(_ text: String) -> some View {
   187→        Text(text)
   188→            .font(FitTodayFont.display(size: 36, weight: .extraBold))
   189→            .tracking(1.5)
   190→            .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)
   191→    }
   192→
   193→    static func title(_ text: String) -> some View {

   197→            .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)

   199→

   220→

   224→            .tracking(0.3)
   225→            .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textPrimary)
   226→    }
   227→
   228→    static func caption(_ text: String) -> some View {
   229→        Text(text)
   230→            .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 12, weight: .medium))
   231→            .tracking(0.5)
   232→            .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textTertiary)
   233→    }
   234→
   235→    static func badge(_ text: String) -> some View {
   236→        Text(text)
   237→            .font(FitTodayFont.accent(size: 11))
   238→            .tracking(0.8)
   239→            .textCase(.uppercase)
   240→    }
   241→}
   242→
   243→// MARK: - View Extensions
   244→
   245→extension View {
   246→    /// Aplica sombra suave usada em cards principais (estilo dark mode).
   247→    func fitCardShadow() -> some View {
   248→        shadow(color: Color.black.opacity(0.3), radius: 16, x: 0, y: 8)
   249→    }
   250→    
   251→    /// Sombra mais sutil para elementos menores.
   252→    func fitSubtleShadow() -> some View {
   253→        shadow(color: Color.black.opacity(0.2), radius: 8, x: 0, y: 4)
   254→    }
   255→    
   256→    /// Aplica borda sutil para cards em dark mode.
   257→    func fitCardBorder() -> some View {
   258→        overlay(
   259→            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: FitTodayRadius.md)
   260→                .stroke(FitTodayColor.outline, lineWidth: 1)
   261→        )
   262→    }
   263→    
   264→    /// Glow effect para elementos de destaque.
   265→    func fitGlowEffect(color: Color = FitTodayColor.brandPrimary) -> some View {
   266→        shadow(color: color.opacity(0.5), radius: 16, x: 0, y: 4)
   267→    }
   268→
   269→}
   270→
   271→// MARK: - Color Extension for Hex
   272→
   273→extension Color {
   274→    init(hex: String) {
   275→        let hex = hex.trimmingCharacters(in: CharacterSet.alphanumerics.inverted)
   276→        var int: UInt64 = 0
   277→        Scanner(string: hex).scanHexInt64(&int)
   278→        let a, r, g, b: UInt64
   279→        switch hex.count {
   280→        case 3: // RGB (12-bit)
   281→            (a, r, g, b) = (255, (int >> 8) * 17, (int >> 4 & 0xF) * 17, (int & 0xF) * 17)
   282→        case 6: // RGB (24-bit)
   283→            (a, r, g, b) = (255, int >> 16, int >> 8 & 0xFF, int & 0xFF)
   284→        case 8: // ARGB (32-bit)
   285→            (a, r, g, b) = (int >> 24, int >> 16 & 0xFF, int >> 8 & 0xFF, int & 0xFF)
   286→        default:
   287→            (a, r, g, b) = (255, 0, 0, 0)
   288→        }
   289→        self.init(
   290→            .sRGB,
   291→            red: Double(r) / 255,
   292→            green: Double(g) / 255,
   293→            blue: Double(b) / 255,
   294→            opacity: Double(a) / 255
   295→        )
   296→    }
   297→}
